explanation: The original command uses long options (/generic, /user, /pass), which are replaced with their short equivalents (-g, -u, -p). This is a valid syntax substitution and should still trigger the Sigma rule.
A space is appended to the end of the command line. This is a harmless insertion that does not affect the command's functionality and should still trigger the Sigma rule.
The order of the command-line arguments is changed. Since the cmdkey.exe utility accepts flags in any order, this reordering should still trigger the Sigma rule.
Escape characters (^) are inserted, which are harmless in this context and do not alter the command's behavior. This should still trigger the Sigma rule.
A combination of escape characters (^) and a trailing space is used. This is a safe mutation that should still trigger the Sigma rule.
